rationale: The citing paper uses Albers, Mantrala, and Sridhar (2010) as more than background: it positions that study as a key prior benchmark for personal-selling short-term elasticities when motivating its own carryover-focused contribution. In the methods section, the authors explicitly state they started database construction from prior meta-analyses including Albers et al. (2010), and Table 4 shows multiple variable codings taken with precedence from that work. In the implications section, they also use the Albers et al. personal-selling elasticity benchmark (0.310) in budget calculations, indicating direct analytical dependence.
relation_description: The citing study treats Albers et al. (2010) as an empirical and methodological foundation for its meta-analysis program. It builds its dataset and coding setup partly from that prior meta-analysis and combines its short-term elasticity benchmark with new carryover estimates for managerial budgeting implications.
